patch.value: GH-608: concurrent edits to different fields always conflict on the shared updated_at scalar (no field-level .toon merge)

Two branches editing disjoint fields of one item merge cleanly (updated_at resolved deterministically, e.g. max-wins merge driver or line-splitting serialization); repro from GH-608 becomes a regression test; documented in merge-semantics docs

GH-608. Disjoint-field concurrent edits (A: title, B: body) always conflict solely on the shared updated_at scalar every pm update rewrites. Most common cooperative case, fails 100%. Distinct from pm-9q2t (count headers) and pm-wc1r (history chain). Fix direction: deterministic updated_at resolution (max-wins merge driver) or serialization that isolates volatile scalars from semantic fields.
